description_long: This is a traditional push broom featuring a long, straight wooden handle attached to a rectangular wooden broom head. The handle is smooth and cylindrical, tapering slightly toward the top, and is securely fixed at the center of the broom head. The broom head is fitted with a dense row of stiff, straight bristles that extend downward, ideal for sweeping large, flat surfaces such as sidewalks or streets. The bristles are arranged in a single, uniform row and appear to be made of a synthetic or natural fiber, with a dark brown coloration. The overall construction is robust, with no visible fasteners or additional attachments, and the finish is matte, giving the broom a utilitarian appearance. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements on any part of the broom.
description: A classic push broom with a long wooden handle and a rectangular head densely packed with straight, dark bristles. The design is simple, sturdy, and well-suited for sweeping urban surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the broom head and the dense, straight bristles extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the broom head and the thickness of the bristle bundle.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the back of the broom head and the bristle arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the broom head and the bristle density.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a round cross-section and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: Bristles are densely packed in a single, straight row along the length of the rectangular broom head, all of uniform length and color, extending directly downward.
attribute_mounting_position: Intended to be stored vertically by leaning against a wall; no visible hole or hook for hanging.
